title: Executive Order 12661—Implementing the Omnibus Trade and Competitiveness Act of 1988 and Related International Trade Matters

flags.justification: Authority is distributed by specific subject matter across many named officials (primarily USTR, with Defense, Treasury, and Commerce receiving narrower slices), each delegation specific and statutorily anchored.

Extremely detailed, section-by-section delegation with specific statutory cross-references, and Sec. 5-101 explicitly retains 'Executive oversight and direction of the President' over all actions taken under this order.
Five distinct Parts cover many trade-policy areas, but all implement one comprehensive statute (the Omnibus Trade Act) plus closely related statutes.
Extensive specificity throughout, including precise timelines for the Toshiba/Kongsberg sanctions and precise membership for interagency bodies.
No incentive structure rewarding behavior contrary to the order's purpose is identifiable.
The specific Toshiba/Kongsberg sanctions have an explicit three-year sunset (Sec. 2-201); the broader delegation framework implements a permanent statute and is evergreen by nature.
Sec. 5-101's explicit presidential-oversight-retention clause and multiple required congressional reports preserve rather than remove accountability.
Extensive mandatory interagency consultation requirements throughout, plus required public and congressional reporting, provide real structured checks.

Comprehensive implementation of the major 1988 trade reform act, including the landmark Toshiba-Kongsberg export-control sanctions responding to the 1987 submarine-technology transfer scandal — genuinely significant.
Careful, section-by-section delegation to specifically named officials with defined roles and dedicated coordinating bodies.

The sanctions-exception process (Sec. 2-201(3)) is narrowly bounded by specific statutory criteria and delegated to specific officials, a standard bounded exception process rather than a broad undermining carve-out.

notes: Validation batch (positive). Structural weight: 1/22 = 4.5%. A massive, comprehensive trade-implementation order that scores very low due to careful, distributed, well-checked design despite its scale and complexity.
